turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512" fill="none">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="gHandle" x1="120" y1="380" x2="240" y2="140" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
      <stop stop-color="#ff8a5b"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#d9533a"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="gShaft" x1="250" y1="200" x2="420" y2="420" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
      <stop stop-color="#cfd8dc"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#90a4ae"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- shadow -->
  <ellipse cx="270" cy="410" rx="120" ry="22" fill="#000" opacity="0.12"/>

  <!-- screwdriver body (rotated) -->
  <g transform="translate(0,0) rotate(-18 260 260)" >
    <!-- handle -->
    <rect x="110" y="300" width="175" height="85" rx="22" fill="url(#gHandle)"/>
    <rect x="130" y="315" width="135" height="55" rx="16" fill="#f39c7a" opacity="0.35"/>

    <!-- handle grip rings -->
    <rect x="150" y="332" width="95" height="10" rx="5" fill="#9b4b3f" opacity="0.35"/>
    <rect x="160" y="360" width="75" height="8" rx="4" fill="#9b4b3f" opacity="0.25"/>

    <!-- shaft -->
    <rect x="258" y="220" width="110" height="255" rx="18" fill="url(#gShaft)"/>

    <!-- metal highlight -->
    <path d="M280 230 C300 265, 350 315, 345 465" stroke="#e9f0f2" stroke-width="8" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.7"/>

    <!-- tip (cross shape silhouette without letters) -->
    <g transform="translate(340 430) rotate(8)">
      <polygon points="0,0 40,-10 40,10" fill="#b0bec5"/>
      <polygon points="-10,0 30,-35 40,-10" fill="#c7d1d6"/>
      <polygon points="-10,0 30,35 40,10" fill="#90a4ae"/>
      <path d="M0,0 L22,-30 L22,30 Z" fill="#a5b7c0" opacity="0.35"/>
    </g>

    <!-- ferrule -->
    <rect x="235" y="275" width="55" height="35" rx="14" fill="#9fb3bf" opacity="0.9"/>
    <rect x="238" y="279" width="49" height="27" rx="12" fill="#bfcad1" opacity="0.55"/>
  </g>

  <!-- small sparkle dots for style -->
  <g opacity="0.35" fill="#fff">
    <circle cx="170" cy="200" r="3"/>
    <circle cx="195" cy="185" r="2"/>
    <circle cx="215" cy="225" r="2.5"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Designing a screwdriver vector**

I need to create a simple vector drawing of a screwdriver without any text. It should include the handle, shaft, and tip, using basic shapes to keep it clean. I want to ensure that there are no letters or words in this illustration. The final output should be in SVG format with a size of 512, and I’ll return it in a JSON structure so it's easy to integrate. Let’s get started on this!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
